records.ety: [OE. tol,tool. AS. tōl; akin to Icel. tōl, Goth. taijan to do, to make, taui deed, work, and perhaps to E. taw to dress leather. √64.]

defs: An instrument such as a hammer, saw, plane, file, and the like, used in the manual arts, to facilitate mechanical operations; any instrument used by a craftsman or laborer at his work; an implement; as, the tools of a joiner, smith, shoe-maker, etc.; also, a cutter, chisel, or other part of an instrument or machine that dresses work.
A machine for cutting or shaping materials; -- also called machine tool.
Hence, any instrument of use or service.
quotes: That angry fool . . . Whipping her horse, did with his smarting tool Oft whip her dainty self.
Him that is aghast of every tool.

defs: A person used as an instrument by another person; -- a word of reproach; as, men of intrigue have their tools, by whose agency they accomplish their purposes.
quotes: I was not made for a minion or a tool.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. tooled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. tooling.]

defs: To shape, form, or finish with a tool.
To travel in a vehicle; to ride or drive.
quotes: Boys on their bicycles tooling along the well-kept roads.
